topUserComplaints: Free tools gate exports behind sign-up walls or add watermarks, making them useless for quick mock-ups.
Comparisons don't show real design output quality—feature lists do not reflect how 'professional' the final graphic looks.
AI tools fail at vector/editable formats; SVG exports often contain embedded raster, defeating purpose for designers.
Licensing terms are unclear or change suddenly, creating legal risk for commercial projects.
Existing comparison sites are outdated, listing dead tools or ignoring 2024-2025 releases like Relume AI, Codia, or Motiff.
recommendedAngles: Interactive comparison tool with side-by-side exemples of the same design brief across tools (logos, UI mockups, social posts).
Curated 2025 'No-sign-up-required' filter showing tools that let you download without email/account
Vector output quality audit: actual test of SVG/EPS exports from each AI tool, with editability score
Commercial license clarity grid: plainly state allowed usage, indemnification, and print-on-demand friendliness
Reddit-powered sentiment timeline: aggregate user satisfaction over time per tool to show stability/decline
